rulings.Case Title: DALE RIGG et al VS. ASTENJOHNSON, INC., 'F/K/A ASTEN INC et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, March 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Eaton Electrical¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability) and 7 (loss of consortium). Mr. Rigg¿s deposition testimony and the declarations of Stanley Dake and William Ewing create a triable issue whether Mr. Rigg was exposed to dust from a Cutler-Hammer product. Defendant¿s interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether the ¿arc shield/chute¿ components of defendant¿s products contained asbestos. Dr Horne¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Rigg¿s exposure to asbestos was a substantial factor in causing his disease. Defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication as to issues 3 (intentional tort), 4 (False Representation), 5 (breach of warranty) and 6 (premises/contractor liability) and 8 (punitive damages) is moot as those causes of action have been dismissed. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DALE RIGG et al VS. ASTENJOHNSON, INC., 'F/K/A ASTEN INC et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, March 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Eaton Electrical Inc. FKA CUTLER-HAMMER INC.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability) and 7 (loss of consortium). Mr. Rigg¿s deposition testimony and the declarations of Stanley Dake and William Ewing create a triable issue whether Mr. Rigg was exposed to dust from a Cutler-Hammer product. Defendant¿s interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether the ¿arc shield/chute¿ components of defendant¿s products contained asbestos. Dr Horne¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Rigg¿s exposure to asbestos was a substantial factor in causing his disease. Defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication as to issues 3 (intentional tort), 4 (False Representation), 5 (breach of warranty) and 6 (premises/contractor liability) and 8 (punitive damages) is moot as those causes of action have been dismissed. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DALE RIGG et al VS. ASTENJOHNSON, INC., 'F/K/A ASTEN INC et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, March 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant General Electric Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability), 7 (loss of consortium) and 8 (punitive damages) is denied. Mr. Rigg¿s deposition testimony and the declarations of Stanley Dake and William Ewing create a triable issue whether Mr. Rigg was exposed to dust from an arc chute component of defendant¿s product. Defendant failed to shift the burden of production on the issue of the asbestos content of arc chutes because its GO 129 interrogatory responses state that some of its switch gears may have contained asbestos components. Dr Horne¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Rigg¿s exposure to asbestos was a substantial factor in causing his disease. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production on the issue of punitive damages because it presents no evidence on the subject. Defendant¿s alternate motion for summary Adjudication as to issues 3 (false representation), 4 (intentional tort), 5 (breach of warranty) and 6 (premises/contractor liability) is moot as those causes of action have been dismissed. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HAROLD DOYLE et al VS. AMERICAN BILTRITE INC et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, March 25, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant H.M. Royal¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 2 (premises or contractor liability) and 3 (punitive damages) and denied as to issues 1 (negligence and strict products liability) and 4 (loss of consortium). The deposition testimony of Mr. Doyle and Mr. Sciortino create a triable issue whether Mr. Doyle was exposed to 9x9 asbestos-containing Amtico tiles manufactured at the plant located at 1985 East State Street in Trenton, New Jersey. Mr Sciortino¿s testimony further creates a triable issue whether the asbestos in the 9x9 tiles was supplied by defendant. Defendant¿s evidentiary objections are overruled. On the issues of punitive damages and premises or contractor Liability, defendant satisfied its burden of production by presenting plaintiffs¿ factually devoid responses to special interrogatories and plaintiffs failed to provide any evidence creating a triable issue. =(220/HEK)
